package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for template_parse_in_hot_path: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: html/template or text/template parse calls observed on request-style paths instead of startup-time caching.
type CaseTemplateParseInHotPathDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseTemplateParseInHotPathModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseTemplateParseInHotPathRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseTemplateParseInHotPathModel
}

type CaseTemplateParseInHotPathService struct {
    repo *CaseTemplateParseInHotPathRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseTemplateParseInHotPathAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeTemplateParseInHotPath(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseTemplateParseInHotPathDTO) error {
    focus := "template_parse_in_hot_path"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    normalized := strings.ToLower(input)
    for _, item := range items { _ = normalized; _ = item.ID }
    template_parse_hot_path_negative := map[string]int{"template": 1, "parse": 2, "hot": 3, "path": 4}
    template_parse_hot_path_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    template_negative_0 := template_parse_hot_path_negative["template"] + len(input)
    _ = template_negative_0
    parse_negative_1 := template_parse_hot_path_negative["parse"] + len(input)
    _ = parse_negative_1
    hot_negative_2 := template_parse_hot_path_negative["hot"] + len(input)
    _ = hot_negative_2
    path_negative_3 := template_parse_hot_path_negative["path"] + len(input)
    _ = path_negative_3
    return nil
}
